title: Solutions Engineer
description: Postman is hiring a Solutions Engineer to partner with enterprise prospects and customers. The role leads technical discovery, maps customer workflows to API platform capabilities, runs product demos, supports proof-of-concept delivery, and partners closely with account teams. Strong candidates can explain API governance, developer workflows, enterprise enablement, and cross-functional rollout planning. Experience working with product, engineering, and customer stakeholders is important.
resume_text: Jay Dreyer is a customer-facing API and AI platform leader. He scaled an API catalog from 30 to 225+ documented APIs, led API governance and OpenAPI standards, managed developer portal strategy, trained product managers on practical AI adoption, built a custom GPT that replaced a 50,000 dollar annual recruiter workflow, and regularly translated technical systems into executive-ready business language. He has led cross-functional initiatives across product, engineering, and operations teams.

title: AI Deployment Engineer
description: OpenAI is hiring an AI Deployment Engineer to help enterprise customers implement generative AI systems in production. The role requires strong technical communication, solutions architecture, API integration experience, deployment planning, and customer-facing delivery. The engineer will partner with account teams, guide implementation programs, and help organizations operationalize AI workflows responsibly.
resume_text: Jay Dreyer has led AI strategy and implementation programs, built a custom GPT that replaced a major recruiting workflow, managed API developer experience, guided enterprise platform adoption, and translated complex technical systems into customer-facing enablement. His background includes API governance, cross-functional delivery, vendor evaluation, and practical enterprise AI rollout planning.

title: Technical Account Manager
description: Datadog is hiring a Technical Account Manager to own strategic customer relationships, guide adoption plans, review architectures, coordinate issue resolution, and present technical recommendations to customer stakeholders. The role requires customer-facing communication, technical problem solving, and the ability to align product capabilities with business goals over time.
resume_text: Jay Dreyer has managed complex technical programs, supported cross-functional stakeholders, created API enablement systems, trained internal teams, and regularly worked across product, engineering, and operations functions. He has customer empathy, executive communication skill, and experience framing technical work in business terms, though he has less direct ownership of a named TAM title.

title: Senior Backend Engineer
description: Glean is hiring a Senior Backend Engineer to build distributed services in Go, design data pipelines, improve Kubernetes-based infrastructure, and own production reliability for backend systems. The role expects deep hands-on software engineering, strong Go experience, container orchestration knowledge, and service performance tuning.
resume_text: Jay Dreyer is strongest in API governance, enterprise enablement, AI strategy, stakeholder communication, program leadership, and developer experience. He has worked closely with engineering organizations and understands platform workflows, but he has not built his career as a hands-on backend engineer in Go or as an infrastructure specialist.

title: Enterprise Account Executive
description: Writer is hiring an Enterprise Account Executive to source and close new business, manage a quota, run executive sales cycles, negotiate commercial terms, and partner with solution consultants on late-stage deals. Strong candidates have direct quota-carrying experience, pipeline management discipline, executive storytelling skill, and comfort selling technical platforms into enterprise accounts.
resume_text: Jay Dreyer is highly credible with enterprise technology, AI adoption, API platform storytelling, and executive communication. He has worked across customer, product, and operations contexts, but his background is not built around direct quota-carrying ownership, commercial negotiation, or full-cycle enterprise closing responsibility.
